adapter_identity.name: Whiskey House Network Management System Adapter

state_transitions.trigger: start() called — initiates REST API + optional WebSocket connections
REST /health/detailed returns OK for all components
REST health check fails 3 consecutive times or WebSocket disconnects
REST health check passes and WebSocket (if configured) reconnects

params.name: nms_api_url
params.description: NMS REST API base URL (e.g., http://localhost:8000/api/v1). FastAPI backend with 75+ endpoints covering devices, topology, SNMP, security, alerts, SPOF detection.

params.name: nms_ws_url
params.description: WebSocket event stream URL (e.g., ws://localhost:8000/api/v1/events/stream). Optional — if not provided, adapter falls back to REST polling only.

params.name: jwt_token
params.description: JWT bearer token for authenticated requests. Optional — NMS may run without auth.

params.name: poll_interval_seconds
params.description: REST polling interval in seconds for device, topology, and alert queries.

params.name: verify_ssl
params.description: Verify SSL certificates for HTTPS connections.

data_sources.description: Discovered network devices (717+). Full inventory with NMS spec fields: vendor, model, role, lifecycle, location. Paginated.
Network interfaces per device: physical, LAG, subinterface, SVI, loopback, tunnel, management.
Physical links from LLDP discovery (411+ deduplicated links). Endpoint-to-endpoint connections.
Subnet-based topology graph from Neo4j. Device nodes with CONNECTS_TO relationships and subnet groupings.
SNMP trap events from network devices. PySNMP UDP listener captures and stores trap data.
SNMP polling metrics per device. Tier-based: critical (30s), high (60s), standard (300s).
Infrastructure alert rules and triggered events. Configurable thresholds per device/metric.
FortiAnalyzer security events: threats, blocks, anomalies. JSON-RPC integration with FortiAnalyzer.
Active single points of failure in network topology. Blast radius calculation per critical device.
Devices with baseline anomalies (suspicious/blocked). Network behavior deviation detection.
Real-time event stream for SNMP traps, poll results, and alert triggers. WebSocket with JSON payloads.

mappings.description: Unique device identifier. Uses device ID when available, falls back to IP address. Primary correlation key for NMS entities.
Entity type: network_device (default), interface, link, trap_event, alert, security_event, spof_detection, baseline_anomaly.
Event type: device_discovery, snmp_trap, infrastructure_alert, security_event, spof_detection, baseline_anomaly, topology_snapshot.
Fixed operation context — all NMS data is network monitoring domain.
Device management IP address. Primary network identifier.
Network role: firewall, switch, router, access_point, server, printer, etc.

mappings.transform: lookup:{FortiGate: net_core, Cisco: net_core, Extreme: it_core, Rockwell: ot_control, PLC: ot_control}
mappings.description: Maps device type/vendor to infrastructure category: net_core, it_core, ot_control, ot_device, it_endpoint.
Whether the device is classified as critical infrastructure. Derived from SPOF analysis (blast_radius > 10) or explicit flag.
Whether the device is an OT (Operational Technology) device: PLCs, SCADA RTUs, HMI panels.
Physical location: MDF, IDF, plant floor, control room. Maps to building/room for physical access tracking.
SNMP polling frequency tier: critical (30s), high (60s), standard (300s). Determines monitoring resolution.
Topological role: upstream (toward internet), downstream (toward endpoints), isolated (no links). Derived from LLDP topology.
Event severity: critical, high, medium, low, info. Applies to traps, alerts, and security events.
Current device health: healthy, degraded, down, unknown. Derived from SNMP polling results.
SPOF blast radius — number of downstream devices affected if this device fails. Higher values indicate more critical infrastructure.
Network baseline compliance: normal, suspicious, blocked. Deviation from established traffic/behavior patterns.

enrichment_rules.description: Auto-classify devices by vendor/model into infrastructure categories (net_core, ot_control, it_endpoint, etc.).
Auto-flag devices as critical when their SPOF blast radius exceeds 10 downstream devices.

metadata.deployment: Docker Compose (PostgreSQL, Neo4j, Redis, Celery Beat)
metadata.cross_module_dependencies: OT Module (equipment topology enrichment), QMS (security compliance events), Support Orchestration (alert-to-incident workflow)
metadata.notes: NMS is the first Python-native spoke — adapter runs directly inside Forge without a TypeScript sidecar. Closest tech stack match to Forge core (Python, FastAPI, Neo4j, TimescaleDB). Graph data (Neo4j device topology) can sync directly to Forge's Neo4j instance. 717+ devices discovered, 411 physical links, 75+ REST endpoints.

change_history.reason: Initial NMS specification drafted from spoke discovery. Used non-standard schema (specification_type format).

D3.8 FTTS compliance: complete rewrite from non-standard specification_type schema to FACTS-compliant 10-section format. Mapped 14 REST endpoints and WebSocket stream to data_sources. Defined 19 context field mappings matching implemented adapter code. Added lifecycle state machine, connection params, error handling, and integrity tracking.
